channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How Ebola kills -- and what it takes to stop it
item.description: It's a virus that can strike with unrelenting force. The kind of care need to knock it out is often not fully available in a lower resource country like the Democratic Republic of Congo.
item.content:encoded:
It's a virus that can strike with unrelenting force. The kind of care need to knock it out is often not fully available in a lower resource country like the Democratic Republic of Congo.

(Image credit: Glody Murhabazi/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: DOJ will pause $1.8 billion fund, per court order. And, key primaries to watch today
item.description: The DOJ says it will abide by a federal court order pausing its anti-weaponization fund. And, six states are holding primaries today. Here are the races to watch.
item.content:encoded:
The DOJ says it will abide by a federal court order pausing its anti-weaponization fund. And, six states are holding primaries today. Here are the races to watch.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Pentagon policy illegally banned transgender troops from military, appeals court rules
item.description: A divided panel of appeals court judges has ruled that a Trump administration policy illegally banned transgender troops from military service.
item.content:encoded:
A divided panel of appeals court judges has ruled that a Trump administration policy illegally banned transgender troops from military service.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
item.description: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.

(Image credit: Jean-Francois Badias)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: For veterans, a place where peace can take root
item.description: Iraq war veteran John Follmer leads vet volunteers who are rehabbing a neglected Japanese garden on the West LA Veterans Affairs Campus.
item.content:encoded:
Iraq war veteran John Follmer leads vet volunteers who are rehabbing a neglected Japanese garden on the West LA Veterans Affairs Campus.

(Image credit: Stella Kalinina for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Tuesday is a big primary day. Here are key races to watch
item.description: Six states — California, Iowa, Montana, New Jersey, South Dakota and New Mexico — hold elections on Tuesday. Here are key races to follow.
item.content:encoded:
Six states — California, Iowa, Montana, New Jersey, South Dakota and New Mexico — hold elections on Tuesday. Here are key races to follow.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Michigan found a way to reduce school vaccine waivers. Until it backfired
item.description: A decade ago, Michigan had high rates of parents not vaccinating their children, so it required them to attend an in-person education class to get an exemption. It worked — until things got ugly.
item.content:encoded:
A decade ago, Michigan had high rates of parents not vaccinating their children, so it required them to attend an in-person education class to get an exemption. It worked — until things got ugly.

(Image credit: Kate Wells)
item.dc:creator: Kate Wells

item.title: Trump vowed to revoke hundreds of citizenships. It's proving harder to do
item.description: President Trump's vow to revoke citizenship worries immigrant advocates, legal scholars and naturalized Americans — but so far it's proving harder to do than the rhetoric suggests.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's vow to revoke citizenship worries immigrant advocates, legal scholars and naturalized Americans — but so far it's proving harder to do than the rhetoric suggests.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: DOJ says it will pause its 'anti-weaponization' fund after judge's ruling, Trump says he urged Israel, Hezbollah to hold fire amid rising tensions over Lebanon, Californians vote in state's primaries.
item.content:encoded: DOJ says it will pause its 'anti-weaponization' fund after judge's ruling, Trump says he urged Israel, Hezbollah to hold fire amid rising tensions over Lebanon, Californians vote in state's primaries.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
item.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.
item.content:encoded:
Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
